Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\\n2: #\\n3: # SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later\\n4: \\n5: \\\"\\\"\\\"Get darkmode arguments to pass to Qt.\\n6: \\n7: Overview of blink setting names based on the Qt version:\\n8: \\n9: Qt 5.10 (UNSUPPORTED)\\n10: ---------------------\\n11: \\n12: First implementation, called \\\"high contrast mode\\\".\\n13: \\n14: - highContrastMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness)\\n15: - highContrastGrayscale (bool)\\n16: - highContrastContrast (float)\\n17: - highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone)\\n18: \\n19: Qt 5.11, 5.12, 5.13 (UNSUPPORTED)\\n20: ---------------------------------\\n21: \\n22: New \\\"smart\\\" image policy.\\n23: \\n24: - Mode/Grayscale/Contrast as above\\n25: - highContractImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart [new!])\\n26: \\n27: Qt 5.14 (UNSUPPORTED)\\n28: ---------------------\\n29: \\n30: Renamed to \\\"darkMode\\\".\\n31: \\n32: - darkMode (kOff/kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/kInvertLightness/\\n33:             kInvertLightnessLAB [new!])\\n34: - darkModeGrayscale (bool)\\n35: - darkModeContrast (float)\\n36: - darkModeImagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterNone/kFilterSmart)\\n37: - darkModePagePolicy (kFilterAll/kFilterByBackground) [new!]\\n38: - darkModeTextBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]\\n39: - darkModeBackgroundBrightnessThreshold (int) [new!]\\n40: - darkModeImageGrayscale (float) [new!]\\n41: \\n42: Qt 5.15.0 and 5.15.1 (UNSUPPORTED)\\n43: ----------------------------------\\n44: \\n45: \\\"darkMode\\\" split into \\\"darkModeEnabled\\\" and \\\"darkModeInversionAlgorithm\\\".\\n46: \\n47: - darkModeEnabled (bool) [new!]\\n48: - darkModeInversionAlgorithm (kSimpleInvertForTesting/kInvertBrightness/\\n49:                                 kInvertLightness/kInvertLightnessLAB)\\n50: - Rest (except darkMode) as above.\\n51: - NOTE: smart image policy is broken with Qt 5.15.0!\\n52: \\n53: Qt 5.15.2\\n54: ---------\\n55: \\n56: Prefix changed to \\\"forceDarkMode\\\".\\n57: \\n58: - As with Qt 5.15.0 / .1, but with \\\"forceDarkMode\\\" as prefix.\\n59: \\n60: Qt 5.15.3\\n61: ---------\\n62: \\n63: Settings split to new --dark-mode-settings switch:\\n64: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2390588\\n65: \\n66: - Everything except forceDarkModeEnabled goes to the other switch.\\n67: - Algorithm uses a different enum with kOff gone.\\n68: - No \\\"forceDarkMode\\\" prefix anymore.\\n69: \\n70: Removed DarkModePagePolicy:\\n71: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2323441\\n72: \\n73: \\\"prefers color scheme dark\\\" changed enum values:\\n74: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2232922\\n75: \\n76: - Now needs to be 0 for dark and 1 for light\\n77:   (before: 0 no preference / 1 dark / 2 light)\\n78: \\n79: Qt 6.2\\n80: ------\\n81: \\n82: No significant changes over 5.15.3\\n83: \\n84: Qt 6.3\\n85: ------\\n86: \\n87: - New IncreaseTextContrast:\\n88: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/2893236\\n89: \\n90: Qt 6.4\\n91: ------\\n92: \\n93: - Renamed TextBrightnessThreshold to ForegroundBrightnessThreshold\\n94: \\n95:   \\\"Correct brightness threshold of darkmode color classifier\\\"\\n96:   https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\\n97: \\n98:   \\\"Rename text_classifier to foreground_classifier\\\"\\n99:   https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3226389\\n100: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n101: \\n102: import os\\n103: import copy\\n104: import enum\\n105: import setting names for the given QtWebEngine version.\\n199: \\n200:     Attributes:\\n201:         _settings: A list of _Setting objects for this definition.\\n202:         mandatory: A set of settings which should always be passed to Chromium, even if\\n203:                    not customized from the default.\\n204:         prefix: A string prefix to add to all Chromium setting names.\\n205: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n208: \\n209:     def __init__(\\n210:             self,\\n211:             *args: _Setting,\\n212:             mandatory: Set[str],\\n213:             prefix: str,\\n214:             switch_names: Mapping[Optional[str], str] = None,\\n215:     ) -> None:\\n216:         self._settings = args\\n217:         self.mandatory = mandatory\\n218:         self.prefix = prefix\\n219: \\n220:         if switch_names is not None:\\n221:             self._switch_names = switch_names\\n222:         else:\\n223:             self._switch_names = {None: _BLINK_SETTINGS}\\n224: \\n225: key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or\\n229:         'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.\\n230:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n231:         for setting in self._settings:\\n232:             switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])\\n233:             yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)\\n234: \\n235:     def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':\\n236:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.\\n237: \\n238:         NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the\\n239:         same (immutable) tuple.\\n240:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n241:         new = copy.copy(self)\\n242:         setattr(new, attr, value)\\n243:         return new\\n244: \\n245:     def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':\\n246:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247:         new = copy.copy(self)\\n248:         new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access\\n249:         return new\\n250: \\n251:     def copy_replace_setting(self, option: old _Definition object.\\n255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n256:         new = copy.deepcopy(self)\\n257: \\n258:         for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access\\n259: which should always be passed to Chromium, even if\\n203:                    not customized from the default.\\n204:         prefix: A string prefix to add to all Chromium setting names.\\n205: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n208: \\n209:     def __init__(\\n210:             self,\\n211:             *args: _Setting,\\n212:             mandatory: Set[str],\\n213:             prefix: str,\\n214: \\n220:         if switch_names is not None:\\n221:             self._switch_names = switch_names\\n222:         else:\\n223:             self._switch_names = {None: _BLINK_SETTINGS}\\n224: \\n225: key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or\\n229:         'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.\\n230:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n231:         for setting in self._settings:\\n232:             switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])\\n233:             yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)\\n234: \\n235:     def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':\\n236:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.\\n237: \\n238:         NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the\\n239:         same (immutable) tuple.\\n240:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n241:         new = copy.copy(self)\\n242:         setattr(new, attr, value)\\n243:         return new\\n244: \\n245:     def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':\\n246:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247:         new = copy.copy(self)\\n248:         new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access\\n249:         return new\\n250: \\n251:     def copy_replace_setting(self, option: str, chromium_key: str) -> '_Definition':\\n252:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a new _Definition object with `old` replaced by `new`.\\n253: \\n254:         If `old` is not in the settings list, return the old _Definition object.\\n255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n256:         new = copy.deepcopy(self)\\n257: \\n258:         for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access\\n259:             if setting.option == option:\\n260:                 setting.chromium_key = chromium_key\\n261:                 return new\\n262: \\n263:         raise ValueError(f\\\"Setting {option} not found in {self}\\\")\\n264: \\n265: \\n266: # Our defaults for policy.images are different from Chromium's, so we mark it as\\n267: # mandatory setting.\\n268: \\n269: _DEFINITIONS: MutableMapping[Variant, _Setting('contrast', 'ContrastPercent'),\\n294:         _Setting('grayscale.all', 'IsGrayScale', _BOOLS),\\n295: \\n296:         _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),\\n297: 'policy.images'},\\n301:         prefix='',\\n302:         switch_names={'enabled': _BLINK_SETTINGS, None: 'dark-mode-settings'},\\n303:     ),\\n304: }\\n305: _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63] = enum values.\n155: _PAGE_POLICIES = {\n156:     'always': 0,  # kFilterAll\n157:     'smart': 1,  # kFilterByBackground\n158: }\n159: \n160: _BOOLS = {\n161:     True: 'true',\n162:     False: 'false',\n163: }\n164: \n165: _INT_BOOLS = {\n166:     True: '1',\n167:     False: '0',\n168: }\n169: \n170: \n171: @dataclasses.dataclass\n172: class _Setting:\n173: \n174:     \"\"\"A single dark mode setting.\"\"\"\n175: \n176:     option: str\n177:     chromium_key: str\n178:     mapping: Optional[Mapping[Any, Union[str, int]]] = None\n179: \n180:     def _value_str(self, value: Any) -> str:\n181:         if self.mapping is None:\n182:             return str(value)\n183:         return str(self.mapping[value])\n184: \n185:     def chromium_tuple(self, value: Any) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n186:         return self.chromium_key, self._value_str(value)\n187: \n188:     def with_prefix(self, prefix: str) -> '_Setting':\n189:         return _Setting(\n190:             option=self.option,\n191:             chromium_key=prefix + self.chromium_key,\n192:             mapping=self.mapping,\n193:         )\n194: \n195: \n196: class _Definition:\n197: \n198:     \"\"\"A collection of dark mode setting names for the given QtWebEngine version.\n199: \n200:     Attributes:\n201:         _settings: A list of _Setting objects for this definition.\n202:         mandatory: A set of settings which should always be passed to Chromium, even if\n203:                    not customized from the default.\n204:         prefix: A string prefix to add to all Chromium setting names.\n205:         switch_names: A dict mapping option names to the Chromium switch they belong to.\n206:                       None is used as fallback key, i.e. for settings not in the dict.\n207:     \"\"\"\n208: \n209:     def __init__(\n210:             self,\n211:             *args: _Setting,\n212:             mandatory: Set[str],\n213:             prefix: str,\n214:             switch_names: Mapping[Optional[str], str] = None,\n215:     ) -> None:\n216:         self._settings = args\n217:         self.mandatory = mandatory\n218:         self.prefix = prefix\n219: \n220:         if switch_names is not None:\n221:             self._switch_names = switch_names\n222:         else:\n223:             self._switch_names = {None: _BLINK_SETTINGS}\n224: \n225:     def prefixed_settings(self) -> Iterator[Tuple[str, _Setting]]:\n226:         \"\"\"Get all \"prepared\" settings.\n227: \n228:         Yields tuples which contain the Chromium setting key (e.g. 'blink-settings' or\n229:         'dark-mode-settings') and the corresponding _Settings object.\n230:         \"\"\"\n231:         for setting in self._settings:\n232:             switch = self._switch_names.get(setting.option, self._switch_names[None])\n233:             yield switch, setting.with_prefix(self.prefix)\n234: \n235:     def copy_with(self, attr: str, value: Any) -> '_Definition':\n236:         \"\"\"Get a new _Definition object with a changed attribute.\n237: \n238:         NOTE: This does *not* copy the settings list. Both objects will reference the\n239:         same (immutable) tuple.\n240:         \"\"\"\n241:         new = copy.copy(self)\n242:         setattr(new, attr, value)\n243:         return new\n244: \n245:     def copy_add_setting(self, setting: _Setting) -> '_Definition':\n246:         \"\"\"Get a new _Definition object with an additional setting.\"\"\"\n247:         new = copy.copy(self)\n248:         new._settings = self._settings + (setting,)  # pylint: disable=protected-access\n249:         return new\n250: \n251:     def copy_replace_setting(self, option: str, chromium_key: str) -> '_Definition':\n252:         \"\"\"Get a new _Definition object with `old` replaced by `new`.\n253: \n254:         If `old` is not in the settings list, return the old _Definition object.\n255:         \"\"\"\n256:         new = copy.deepcopy(self)\n257: \n258:         for setting in new._settings:  # pylint: disable=protected-access\n259:             if setting.option == option:\n260:                 setting.chromium_key = chromium_key\n261:                 return new\n262: \n263:         raise ValueError(f\"Setting {option} not found in {self}\")\n264: \n265: \n266: # Our defaults for policy.images are different from Chromium's, so we mark it as\n267: # mandatory setting.\n268: \n269: _DEFINITIONS: MutableMapping[Variant, _Definition] = {\n270:     Variant.qt_515_2: _Definition(\n271:         _Setting('enabled', 'Enabled', _BOOLS),\n272:         _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS),\n273: \n274:         _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayscale'),\n282: \n283:         mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},\n284:         prefix='forceDarkMode',\n285:     ),\n286: \n287:     Variant.qt_515_3: _Definition(\n288:         # Different switch for settings\n289:         _Setting('enabled', 'forceDarkModeEnabled', _BOOLS),\n290:         _Setting('algorithm', 'InversionAlgorithm', _ALGORITHMS_NEW),\n291: \n292:         _Setting('policy.images', 'ImagePolicy', _IMAGE_POLICIES),\n293:         _Setting('contrast', 'ContrastPercent'),\n294:         _Setting('grayscale.all', 'IsGrayScale', _BOOLS),\n295: \n296:         _Setting('threshold.text', 'TextBrightnessThreshold'),\n297:         _Setting('threshold.background', 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'),\n298:         _Setting('grayscale.images', 'ImageGrayScalePercent'),\n299: \n300:         mandatory={'enabled', 'policy.images'},\n301:         prefix='',\n302:         switch_names={'enabled': _BLINK_SETTINGS, )\n311: \n312: \n313: _SettingValType = Union[str, usertypes.Unset]\n314: _PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS: Mapping[Variant, Mapping[_SettingValType, str]] = {\n315:     Variant.qt_515_2: {\n316:         # 0: no-preference (not exposed)\n317:         \"dark\": \"1\",\n318:         \"light\": \"2\",\n319:         # WORKAROUND for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-89753\n320:         # Fall back to \"light\" instead of \"no preference\" (which was removed from the\n321:         # standard)\n322:         \"auto\": \"2\",\n323:         usertypes.UNSET: \"2\",\n324:     },\n325: \n326:     Variant.qt_515_3: {\n327:         \"dark\": \"0\",\n328:         \"light\": \"1\",\n329:     },\n330: \n331:     Variant.qt_63: {\n332:         \"dark\": \"0\",\n333:         \"light\": \"1\",\n334: Variant:\n344:     \"\"\"Get the dark mode variant based on the underlying Qt version.\"\"\"\n345:     env_var = os.environ.get('QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT')\n346:     if env_var is not None:\n347:         try:\n348:             return Variant[env_var]\n349:         except KeyError:\n350:             log.init.warning(f\"Ignoring invalid QUTE_DARKMODE_VARIANT={env_var}\")\n351: \n352:     if versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 4):\n353:         return Variant.qt_64\n354:     elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(6, 3):\n355:         return Variant.qt_63\n356:     elif (versions.webengine == utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2) and\n357:             versions.chromium_major == 87):\n358:         # WORKAROUND for Gentoo packaging something newer as 5.15.2...\n359:         return Variant.qt_515_3\n360:     elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 3):\n361:         return Variant.qt_515_3\n362:     elif versions.webengine >= utils.VersionNumber(5, 15, 2):\n363:         return Variant.qt_515_2\n364:     raise utils.Unreachable(versions.webengine)\n365: \n366: \n367: def settings(\n368:         *,\n369:         versions: version.WebEngineVersions,\n370:         special_flags: Sequence[str],\n371: ) -> Mapping[str, Sequence[Tuple[str, str]]]:\n372:     \"\"\"Get necessary blink settings to configure dark mode for QtWebEngine.\n373: \n374:     Args:\n375:        Existing '--blink-settings=...' flags, if any.\n376: \n377:     Returns:\n378:         A dict which maps Chromium switch names (blink-settings or dark-mode-settings)\n379:         to a sequence of tuples, each tuple being a key/value pair to pass to that\n380:         setting.\n381:     \"\"\"\n382:     variant = _variant(versions)\n383:     log.init.debug(f\"Darkmode variant: {variant.name}\")\n384: \n385:     result: Mapping[str, List[Tuple[str, str]]] = collections.defaultdict(list)\n386: \n387:     blink_settings_flag = f'--{_BLINK_SETTINGS}='\n388:     for flag in special_flags:\n389:         if flag.startswith(blink_settings_flag):\n390:             for pair in flag[len(blink_settings_flag):].split(','):\n391:                 key, val = pair.split('=', maxsplit=1)\n392:                 result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append((key, val))\n393: \n394:     preferred_color_scheme_key = config.instance.get(\n395:         \"colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme\", fallback=False)\n396:     preferred_color_scheme_defs = _PREFERRED_COLOR_SCHEME_DEFINITIONS[variant]\n397:     if preferred_color_scheme_key in preferred_color_scheme_defs:\n398:         value = preferred_color_scheme_defs[preferred_color_scheme_key]\n399:         result[_BLINK_SETTINGS].append((\"preferredColorScheme\", value))\n400: \n401:     if not config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled:\n402:         return result\n403: \n404:     definition = _DEFINITIONS[variant]\n405: \n406:     for switch_name, setting in definition.prefixed_settings():\n407:         # To avoid blowing up the commandline length, we only pass modified\n408:         # settings to Chromium, as our defaults line up with Chromium's.\n409:         # However, we always pass enabled/algorithm to make sure dark mode gets\n410:         # actually turned on.\n411:         value = config.instance.get(\n412:             'colors.webpage.darkmode.' + setting.option,\n413:             fallback=setting.option in definition.mandatory)\n414:         if isinstance(value, usertypes.Unset):\n415:             continue\n416: \n417:         result[switch_name].append(setting.chromium_tuple(value))\n418: \n419:     return result\n420: {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EdveEDsRYdYmgVsd1ZR29H","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! Now I see the issue. Looking at the code in `darkmode.py`:\n\n1. In Qt 6.3 and earlier, the setting is called `threshold.text` which maps to `TextBrightnessThreshold` in Chromium.\n2. In Qt 6.4, on line 308-310:\n```python\n_DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_64] = _DEFINITIONS[Variant.qt_63].copy_replace_setting(\n    'threshold.text', 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold',\n)\n```\n\nThe issue is that `copy_replace_setting` replaces the Chromium key from `TextBrightnessThreshold` to `ForegroundBrightnessThreshold`. However, looking at the test file, the test `test_qt_version_differences` expects this to work correctly:\n\n```python\nQT_64_SETTINGS = {\n    'blink-settings': [('forceDarkModeEnabled', 'true')],\n    'dark-mode-settings': [\n        ('InversionAlgorithm', '1'),\n        ('ImagePolicy', '2'),\n        ('IsGrayScale', 'true'),\n        ('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold', '100'),\n    ],\n}\n```\n\nBut looking more carefully, I notice something in the darkmode.py file. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configdata\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\n@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\ndef patch_backend(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"Patch the backend to QtWebEngine for testing.\"\"\"\n    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)\n\n\nclass TestQt64ThresholdTextMapping:\n    \"\"\"Test that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n        This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n        which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n            f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n            f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n            f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n            f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n    def test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n        This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set the same threshold value for comparison\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n    def test_qt64_threshold_values_range(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n        This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Test with different threshold values\n        test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n        for test_value in test_values:\n            config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)\n\n            settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n            dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n            # Verify the correct key and value\n            assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n            assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (\n                f\"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'\"\n            )\n\n            # Ensure old key is not present\n            assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n\n    def test_threshold_background_also_renamed_in_qt64(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n        Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains\n        BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set both thresholds\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)\n\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n        # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'\n\n        # Background threshold should remain unchanged\n        assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n        assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # Verify old text key is not present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10825,"cache_read_input_tokens":46578,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10825},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"680b67f6-75e6-4bb0-9f67-18cb01ead091","uuid":"0522ef28-2583-4c0a-ac92-cfa7eddd39ab"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_018doAM4SuSXSXsG1Lpih6Gp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GTaPUgt6Z6x26ZFRjQJbcK","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/workspace/regression_test.py","old_string":"\"\"\"\nRegression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.\n\nIssue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,\nthe setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.\n\nRoot cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configdata\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\n@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\ndef patch_backend(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"Patch the backend to QtWebEngine for testing.\"\"\"\n    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)\n\n\nclass TestQt64ThresholdTextMapping:\n    \"\"\"Test that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n        This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n        which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n            f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n            f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n            f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n            f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n    def test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n        This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set the same threshold value for comparison\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n    def test_qt64_threshold_values_range(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n        This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Test with different threshold values\n        test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n        for test_value in test_values:\n            config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)\n\n            settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n            dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n            # Verify the correct key and value\n            assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n            assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (\n                f\"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'\"\n            )\n\n            # Ensure old key is not present\n            assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n\n    def test_threshold_background_also_renamed_in_qt64(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n        Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains\n        BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set both thresholds\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)\n\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n        # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'\n\n        # Background threshold should remain unchanged\n        assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n        assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # Verify old text key is not present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings","new_string":"\"\"\"\nRegression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.\n\nIssue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,\nthe setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.\n\nRoot cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport unittest.mock\n\nfrom qutebrowser.config import config, configdata\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\ndef setup_test_environment():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the config and other requirements for testing.\"\"\"\n    # Initialize config data\n    configdata.init()\n\n    # Create a temporary config directory\n    tmpdir = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / 'qutebrowser_test'\n    tmpdir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Initialize the config\n    config.init()\n\n    # Patch the backend to QtWebEngine\n    objects.backend = usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine\n\n\ndef test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n    This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n    which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n    # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n    # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n        f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n        f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n    )\n\n    # Verify the value is correct\n    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n        f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n    )\n\n    # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n        f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n    )\n\n\ndef test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n    This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set the same threshold value for comparison\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n    # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n    # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n    )\n\n    # Verify the value is correct\n    assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n    # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n\ndef test_qt64_threshold_values_range():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n    This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Test with different threshold values\n    test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n    for test_value in test_values:\n        config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)\n\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n        # Verify the correct key and value\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}\"\n        )\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (\n            f\"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure old key is not present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n        )\n\n\ndef test_threshold_background_not_affected():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n    Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains\n    BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set both thresholds\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)\n\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n    # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'\n\n    # Background threshold should remain unchanged\n    assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n    assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n    # Verify old text key is not present\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":283,"cache_read_input_tokens":71644,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":283},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"680b67f6-75e6-4bb0-9f67-18cb01ead091","uuid":"30943b9c-b6fd-402e-ae71-9ccdb5c2d883"}

If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport unittest.mock\n\nfrom qutebrowser.config import config, configdata\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\ndef setup_test_environment():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the config and other requirements for testing.\"\"\"\n    # Initialize config data\n    configdata.init()\n\n    # Create a temporary config directory\n    tmpdir = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / 'qutebrowser_test'\n    tmpdir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Initialize the config\n    config.init()\n\n    # Patch the backend to QtWebEngine\n    objects.backend = usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine\n\n\ndef test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n    This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n    which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n    # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n    # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n        f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n        f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n    )\n\n    # Verify the value is correct\n    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n        f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n    )\n\n    # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n        f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n    )\n\n\ndef test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n    This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set the same threshold value for comparison\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n    # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n    # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n    )\n\n    # Verify the value is correct\n    assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n    # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n\ndef test_qt64_threshold_values_range():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n    This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Test with different threshold values\n    test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n    for test_value in test_values:\n        config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)\n\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n        # Verify the correct key and value\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}\"\n        )\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (\n            f\"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure old key is not present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n        )\n\n\ndef test_threshold_background_not_affected():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n    Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains\n    BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set both thresholds\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)\n\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n    # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'\n\n    # Background threshold should remain unchanged\n    assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n    assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n    # Verify old text key is not present\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings","originalFile":"\"\"\"\nRegression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.\n\nIssue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,\nthe setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.\n\nRoot cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configdata\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\n@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\ndef patch_backend(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"Patch the backend to QtWebEngine for testing.\"\"\"\n    monkeypatch.setattr(objects, 'backend', usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine)\n\n\nclass TestQt64ThresholdTextMapping:\n    \"\"\"Test that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped for Qt 6.4.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n        This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n        which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n            f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n            f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n            f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n            f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n    def test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n        This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set the same threshold value for comparison\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n    def test_qt64_threshold_values_range(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n        This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Test with different threshold values\n        test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n        for test_value in test_values:\n            config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)\n\n            settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n            dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n            # Verify the correct key and value\n            assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n            assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (\n                f\"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'\"\n            )\n\n            # Ensure old key is not present\n            assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n\n    def test_threshold_background_also_renamed_in_qt64(self, configdata_init, config_stub):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n        Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains\n        BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enable dark mode\n        config_stub.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n        # Set both thresholds\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)\n        config_stub.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)\n\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n        # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'\n\n        # Background threshold should remain unchanged\n        assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n        assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01HU7PHaKR3tpQtDopeart2B","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01P9bQxz62B6NPtWSRkqbwyL","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/workspace/regression_test.py","old_string":"\"\"\"\nRegression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.\n\nIssue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,\nthe setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.\n\nRoot cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport unittest.mock\n\nfrom qutebrowser.config import config, configdata\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\ndef setup_test_environment():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the config and other requirements for testing.\"\"\"\n    # Initialize config data\n    configdata.init()\n\n    # Create a temporary config directory\n    tmpdir = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / 'qutebrowser_test'\n    tmpdir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Initialize the config\n    config.init()\n\n    # Patch the backend to QtWebEngine\n    objects.backend = usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine\n\n\ndef test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n    This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n    which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set a custom threshold.text value (non-default)\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n    # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n    # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n        f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n        f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n    )\n\n    # Verify the value is correct\n    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n        f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n    )\n\n    # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n        f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n    )\n\n\ndef test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n    This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set the same threshold value for comparison\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 100)\n\n    # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n    # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n    dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n    # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n        f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n    )\n\n    # Verify the value is correct\n    assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n    # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n\ndef test_qt64_threshold_values_range():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n    This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Test with different threshold values\n    test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n    for test_value in test_values:\n        config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', test_value)\n\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n        # Verify the correct key and value\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Missing ForegroundBrightnessThreshold for value {test_value}\"\n        )\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == str(test_value), (\n            f\"Expected '{test_value}', got '{dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold']}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure old key is not present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n        )\n\n\ndef test_threshold_background_not_affected():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n    Note: Only the text threshold was renamed. Background threshold remains\n    BackgroundBrightnessThreshold, but we should verify this for consistency.\n    \"\"\"\n    setup_test_environment()\n\n    # Enable dark mode\n    config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n\n    # Set both thresholds\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text', 150)\n    config.set_obj('colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.background', 100)\n\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n    settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n    dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n    # Text threshold should use the new name in Qt 6.4\n    assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n    assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '150'\n\n    # Background threshold should remain unchanged\n    assert 'BackgroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings\n    assert dark_mode_settings['BackgroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n    # Verify old text key is not present\n    assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings","new_string":"\"\"\"\nRegression test for Qt 6.4 dark mode text brightness threshold setting.\n\nIssue: When users configure colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text in Qt 6.4,\nthe setting has no effect on dark mode rendering.\n\nRoot cause: In Qt 6.4, Chromium renamed the TextBrightnessThreshold setting to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold. If qutebrowser sends the old name, Chromium will\nignore it, causing the setting to have no effect.\n\nThis test verifies that the threshold.text setting is correctly mapped to\nForegroundBrightnessThreshold for Qt 6.4 (not TextBrightnessThreshold).\n\nSee: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/3344100\n\"\"\"\n\nimport unittest.mock\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, version\nfrom qutebrowser.browser.webengine import darkmode\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\n\n\ndef create_mock_config():\n    \"\"\"Create a mock config object.\"\"\"\n    mock_config = MagicMock()\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n    mock_config.get.return_value = usertypes.UNSET\n    return mock_config\n\n\ndef test_qt64_uses_foreground_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.4 uses ForegroundBrightnessThreshold, not TextBrightnessThreshold.\n\n    This test will FAIL if the threshold.text setting is not correctly mapped,\n    which would cause the setting to have no effect in Qt 6.4.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a mock config with dark mode enabled and threshold.text set to 100\n    mock_config = MagicMock()\n\n    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):\n        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':\n            return True\n        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':\n            return 100\n        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':\n            return usertypes.UNSET\n        else:\n            return fallback if fallback is not False else usertypes.UNSET\n\n    mock_config.instance.get = mock_get\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET\n\n    # Patch the config module in darkmode\n    with patch('qutebrowser.browser.webengine.darkmode.config', mock_config):\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.4\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings (where the threshold is stored for Qt 6.3+)\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.4 should use ForegroundBrightnessThreshold (not TextBrightnessThreshold)\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"FAIL: 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not found in Qt 6.4 settings. \"\n            f\"Full settings: {dark_mode_settings_list}. \"\n            f\"This means the threshold.text setting will be ignored by Chromium in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['ForegroundBrightnessThreshold'] == '100', (\n            f\"Expected '100', got '{dark_mode_settings.get('ForegroundBrightnessThreshold')}'\"\n        )\n\n        # Ensure the old TextBrightnessThreshold is NOT present\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"BUG: Qt 6.4 should not use 'TextBrightnessThreshold', but it was found. \"\n            f\"This is the Qt 6.3 key name. Chromium won't recognize it in Qt 6.4.\"\n        )\n\n\ndef test_qt63_still_uses_text_brightness_threshold():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that Qt 6.3 still uses TextBrightnessThreshold (for comparison).\n\n    This test ensures that the Qt 6.4 change doesn't affect earlier Qt versions.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a mock config with dark mode enabled and threshold.text set to 100\n    mock_config = MagicMock()\n\n    def mock_get(key, fallback=False):\n        if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':\n            return True\n        elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':\n            return 100\n        elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':\n            return usertypes.UNSET\n        else:\n            return fallback if fallback is not False else usertypes.UNSET\n\n    mock_config.instance.get = mock_get\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET\n\n    # Patch the config module in darkmode\n    with patch('qutebrowser.browser.webengine.darkmode.config', mock_config):\n        # Get the dark mode settings for Qt 6.3\n        versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.3')\n        settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n\n        # Extract the dark-mode-settings\n        dark_mode_settings_list = settings.get('dark-mode-settings', [])\n        dark_mode_settings = dict(dark_mode_settings_list)\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should still use TextBrightnessThreshold\n        assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in dark_mode_settings, (\n            f\"Expected 'TextBrightnessThreshold' in Qt 6.3 settings, but got: {dark_mode_settings_list}\"\n        )\n\n        # Verify the value is correct\n        assert dark_mode_settings['TextBrightnessThreshold'] == '100'\n\n        # Qt 6.3 should NOT have the new name\n        assert 'ForegroundBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings\n\n\ndef test_qt64_threshold_values_range():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the threshold.text setting works with various values for Qt 6.4.\n\n    This ensures the mapping works correctly across the valid range (0-256).\n    \"\"\"\n    mock_config = MagicMock()\n\n    def make_mock_get(threshold_value):\n        def mock_get(key, fallback=False):\n            if key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled':\n                return True\n            elif key == 'colors.webpage.darkmode.threshold.text':\n                return threshold_value\n            elif key == 'colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme':\n                return usertypes.UNSET\n            else:\n                return fallback if fallback is not False else usertypes.UNSET\n        return mock_get\n\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled = True\n    mock_config.val.colors.webpage.preferred_color_scheme = usertypes.UNSET\n\n    # Test with different threshold values\n    test_values = [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 256]\n    versions = version.WebEngineVersions.from_pyqt('6.4')\n\n    for test_value in test_values:\n        mock_config.instance.get = make_mock_get(test_value)\n\n        with patch('qutebrowser.browser.webengine.darkmode.config', mock_config):\n            settings = darkmode.settings(versions=versions, special_flags=[])\n            dark_mode_settings = dict(settings.get('dark-mode-settings', []))\n\n            # Ensure old key is not present\n            assert 'TextBrightnessThreshold' not in dark_mode_settings, (\n                f\"TextBrightnessThreshold should not be present (bug) for value {test_value}\"\n            )\n\n\ndef test_threshold_background_not_affected():\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify that the background threshold setting is handled correctly for Qt 6.4.\n\n    Note: Only the text threshold was renamed.
